title: Rename constant across 5 ripple locations (easy propagation)
goal: Rename the constant DEFAULT_TIMEOUT to REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS across the entire codebase. The value must also change from 30 (seconds) to 30000 (milliseconds). Update all imports, references, default parameter values, and usages in config.py, http.py, retry.py, pool.py, monitor.py, and __init__.py. All tests in test_netlib.py must pass.
hints: All 6 source files under netlib/ reference DEFAULT_TIMEOUT. Change both the name and the value.

seed_files.netlib/__init__.py:
from .config import DEFAULT_TIMEOUT
from .http import HttpClient
from .retry import RetryPolicy
from .pool import ConnectionPool
from .monitor import HealthChecker
seed_files.netlib/config.py:
# Network configuration constants
DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 30  # seconds
MAX_RETRIES = 3
POOL_SIZE = 10
KEEP_ALIVE = True
seed_files.tests/test_netlib.py:
import pytest
from netlib.config import REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS
from netlib.http import HttpClient
from netlib.retry import RetryPolicy
from netlib.pool import ConnectionPool
from netlib.monitor import HealthChecker


def test_config_value():
    assert REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS == 30000

def test_http_default_timeout():
    client = HttpClient("http://example.com")
    assert client.timeout == 30000

def test_http_custom_timeout():
    client = HttpClient("http://example.com", timeout=5000)
    assert client.timeout == 5000

def test_http_get():
    client = HttpClient("http://example.com")
    result = client.get("/api")
    assert result["timeout"] == 30000

def test_retry_default_timeout():
    policy = RetryPolicy()
    assert policy.timeout == 30000

def test_pool_default_timeout():
    pool = ConnectionPool("example.com")
    assert pool.timeout == 30000

def test_pool_stats():
    pool = ConnectionPool("example.com")
    stats = pool.stats()
    assert stats["timeout"] == 30000

def test_monitor_default_timeout():
    checker = HealthChecker(["http://example.com"])
    assert checker.timeout == 30000

def test_monitor_check():
    checker = HealthChecker(["http://example.com"])
    result = checker.check("http://example.com")
    assert result["timeout"] == 30000

def test_init_import():
    from netlib import REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS as timeout
    assert timeout == 30000

ground_truth: Must update 6 files: config.py (rename constant and change value from 30 to 30000), __init__.py (update import), http.py (update import and default parameter), retry.py (update import and default parameter), pool.py (update import and default parameter), monitor.py (update import and default parameter). All 10 tests verify the new name and value.

eval_spec.check_script_content:
#!/bin/bash
cd "$WORKDIR"
set +e
SCORE=0
TOTAL=7

# Check 1: Tests pass
OUTPUT=$(python3 -m pytest tests/test_netlib.py -v 2>&1)
PASSED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -c " PASSED")
if [ "$PASSED" -eq 10 ]; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: All 10 tests pass"
else
    echo "  FAIL: $PASSED/10 tests passed"
fi

# Check 2: No DEFAULT_TIMEOUT in any source file
COUNT=$(grep -r "DEFAULT_TIMEOUT" netlib/ --include="*.py" | grep -v __pycache__ | wc -l)
if [ "$COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: No DEFAULT_TIMEOUT references remain"
else
    echo "  FAIL: $COUNT references to DEFAULT_TIMEOUT remain"
fi

# Check 3: REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS in config.py
if grep -q "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 30000" netlib/config.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: config.py has REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 30000"
else
    echo "  FAIL: config.py missing REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 30000"
fi

# Check 4: __init__.py imports REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS
if grep -q "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS" netlib/__init__.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: __init__.py imports REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS"
else
    echo "  FAIL: __init__.py missing REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS import"
fi

# Check 5: http.py uses REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS
if grep -q "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS" netlib/http.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: http.py uses REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS"
else
    echo "  FAIL: http.py missing REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS"
fi

# Check 6: retry.py uses REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS
if grep -q "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS" netlib/retry.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: retry.py uses REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS"
else
    echo "  FAIL: retry.py missing REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS"
fi

# Check 7: pool.py uses REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS
if grep -q "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS" netlib/pool.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: pool.py uses REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS"
else
    echo "  FAIL: pool.py missing REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS"
fi

echo ""
echo "Score: $SCORE/$TOTAL"

if [ "$SCORE" -ge 7 ]; then
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi
